auth.loginTitle: A barbershop with serious control from day one.
auth.loginDescription: Sign in against the real backend. The JWT is stored in an httpOnly cookie and the dashboard runs as a Server Component.
auth.signupTitle: Create tenant, initial branch and admin in one step.
auth.signupDescription: Signup delegates the whole onboarding flow to the backend and opens the session automatically with the frontend secure cookie.
auth.loginCardDescription: Sign in with email + password. The backend resolves the tenant from the user.
auth.signupCardDescription: This flow creates the tenant, branch, admin and initial access. When it finishes, you land directly in the dashboard.
auth.signupFootnote: Signup calls the real backend and keeps you authenticated without exposing the token in the browser.
auth.sessionExpired: Your session expired. Sign in again.

errors.unauthorized: Your session is no longer valid. Sign in again.
errors.forbidden: You don't have permission to perform this action.
errors.branchRequired: Select a branch above to continue.
errors.branchForbidden: You don't have access to the selected branch.
errors.conflict: A record with those values already exists.
errors.validation: Review the data and try again.

ui.searchPlaceholder: Search will arrive with appointments, cash and scheduling
ui.branchHint: Branch-scoped via cookie and internal BFF.
ui.branchUpdateFailed: We couldn't save the selected branch.
ui.workspaceDescription: Additional actions, admin modules and branch switching.

dashboard.title: Context ready for operations
dashboard.errorTitle: We couldn't load your current context
dashboard.errorFallback: The backend returned an unexpected error while calling /me.
dashboard.sessionDescription: Authenticated session for {email}. This dashboard consumes /api/v1/me server-side and never exposes the token to the browser.
dashboard.contextDescription: BarberSuite is already connected to the real backend and ready for recruiter demos.
dashboard.branchesDescription: The list comes from user_branch_access and respects the tenant from the JWT.
dashboard.branchesEmpty: This user does not have any branches assigned yet.

services.pageDescription: This view operates against /api/services and respects tenant scope.
services.description: Tenant-scoped management with active-first ordering and quick editing in place.
services.listDescription: Services are displayed with active ones first and name ascending.
services.emptyDescription: Create the first one to start operating the tenant.
services.selectActionDescription: You can create a new service or open an existing one to edit it and change its state.
services.name: Name
services.loadOneFailed: We couldn't load the requested service.
services.toggleFailed: We couldn't change the service status.
services.conflict: A service with that name already exists within this tenant.

form.description: Changes go through the internal BFF and keep the token out of the browser.
form.newDefaultActive: New services are created active by default.
form.nameValidation: The name must be at least 2 characters long.
form.durationValidation: Duration must be between 5 and 480 minutes.
form.priceValidation: Price must be greater than or equal to 0.

clients.pageDescription: This view uses /api/clients and depends on the branch selected in bs_branch_id.
clients.missingBranchTitle: Select a branch above to continue.
clients.missingBranchDescription: Clients are branch-scoped. Until there is an active branch in the cookie, this screen does not make requests.
clients.description: The list uses the branch selected above and supports search and pagination.
clients.listDescription: Search by name, phone or email with branch-scoped pagination.
clients.searchEmptyTitle: We couldn't find clients for that query.
clients.searchEmptyDescription: Try another term or create the first client for this branch.
clients.emptyDescription: Register the first client for the selected branch.
clients.selectActionDescription: You can create a new client, edit an existing one or change its status from the table.
clients.name: Name
clients.loadOneFailed: We couldn't load the requested client.
clients.toggleFailed: We couldn't change the client status.

form.description: This action uses the internal BFF and attaches the branch from the selected cookie.
form.newDefaultActive: New clients are created active by default.
form.fullNameValidation: Full name must be at least 2 characters long.
form.emailValidation: Enter a valid email or leave the field empty.

appointments.title: Scheduling ready for the next slice
appointments.description: This module is reserved for the operational calendar and does not run real flows yet.

bullets.one: Slots, availability and barber assignment will live here.
bullets.two: The shell already supports mobile navigation and breadcrumbs for this flow.
bullets.three: When the scheduling slice lands, this screen will stop being a placeholder.

receipts.title: Cash register ready for the fiscal slice
receipts.description: Receipts and numbering remain pending, but the shell is already prepared to host them.

bullets.one: It will integrate with receipt_sequences and branch daily close.
bullets.two: Mobile access already prioritizes this module for operational roles.
bullets.three: Empty states already keep the final product look and feel.

branches.title: Branch administration is evolving
branches.description: The admin module already has a place in the shell and will grow on top of the existing branches slice.

bullets.one: Policies, schedules and branch-specific settings will live here.
bullets.two: The selector above remains the source of truth for branch-scoped flows.
bullets.three: The design already covers desktop and mobile without reworking the chrome.

settings.title: System ready for internal adjustments
settings.description: This section will centralize operational preferences, branding and tenant controls.

bullets.one: You can later use it for schedules, branding and global preferences.
bullets.two: Theme and locale switching already anticipate part of that future panel.
bullets.three: Keeping this space from now avoids shell redesigns later.
